title: Impeaching John James McConnell Jr., Chief Judge of the United States District Court for the District of Rhode Island, for high crimes and misdemeanors.
plain_summary: This bill tries to impeach a federal judge named John McConnell from Rhode Island. Congress is accusing him of two serious problems with how he does his job as a judge. First, they say he lets his personal political opinions affect his court decisions instead of being fair and neutral. Second, they claim he has a conflict of interest because he sits on the board of a group that gets money from the government while he also makes decisions in court cases involving that same government.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution calls for the impeachment of John James McConnell Jr. He is the Chief Judge of the United States District Court for the District of Rhode Island. The House of Representatives accuses him of high crimes and misdemeanors.

sections.title: Article I: Abuse of Power
sections.text: The first charge claims that Chief Judge McConnell broke his oath to uphold the Constitution. Instead of being neutral and fair, he used his position as a judge to advance his own political beliefs. The Constitution says that the House of Representatives has the sole power to impeach federal judges and other civil officers. It also gives the President broad authority over the Executive Branch. Chief Judge McConnell is accused of putting his political views ahead of his duty to be impartial in a court case called State of New York v. Donald Trump. Throughout his career, McConnell has shown his political views through financial donations and public statements. The Code of Conduct for United States Judges says under Canon 5 that judges should stay out of political activities. McConnell served as director of the Rhode Island branch of Planned Parenthood. He and his wife gave almost seven hundred thousand dollars in political donations to Democratic committees, causes, and campaigns. The resolution claims McConnell let his personal political opinions influence his court decisions and rulings. In January 2021, McConnell made several public comments that showed bias. He said judges do have opinions and that this idea that they should have no opinions is not true. He compared President Trump's first term to the Civil War and Jim Crow laws and called him a tyrant. He made comments about how the law applies differently to criminals who are women, black, or transgender. He talked about how privileged white males need to understand people who may be women, black, transgender, poor, or rich when sentencing them. He said they formed a committee to look at race in their court and whether they gave different sentences to blacks and whites. The resolution argues that McConnell's financial contributions and public comments show a political bias that would prevent him from making fair judgments about President Trump and his administration.

sections.title: Article II: Conflicts of Interest
sections.text: The second charge claims that Chief Judge McConnell had a major conflict of interest that prevented him from being impartial in the State of New York v. Donald Trump case. There is evidence that McConnell serves on the Board of Directors for a nonprofit organization called Crossroads Rhode Island. He previously served as Chair of the Board from 2011 to 2021. According to tax filings, McConnell has appeared every year as a director since he became a federal judge. In 2023, Crossroads Rhode Island had total revenue of thirty million six hundred sixty four thousand seven hundred seventy eight dollars. Of this amount, eighteen million six hundred sixteen thousand eight hundred seventy four dollars came from various government entities including the Government of Rhode Island. In fiscal year 2025, Crossroads Rhode Island has already received two million eight hundred ninety seven thousand six hundred thirty dollars and forty one cents from the Rhode Island state government. The State Government of Rhode Island is a plaintiff in the lawsuit against President Trump that McConnell presided over. In his court decision, McConnell stated that all states rely on federal funds to provide and maintain vital programs and services. The resolution argues there is a clear conflict of interest because McConnell presided over and made a decision about a case that significantly impacts funding for an organization where he serves as a director. The Code of Conduct for United States Judges says under Canon 2A that judges must avoid all impropriety and appearance of impropriety. Section 455 of title 28 of the United States Code requires that judges must remove themselves from any proceeding where their impartiality might reasonably be questioned. This same law requires judges to remove themselves when they know they have a financial interest in the subject matter or in a party to the proceeding, or any other interest that could be substantially affected by the outcome. The resolution claims McConnell had a significant conflict of interest due to his role as a director of Crossroads Rhode Island. This conflict made it impossible for him to be impartial. McConnell should have immediately removed himself from the case but instead continued to oversee it while knowing he was violating his duty of impartiality under federal law.
